H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) stands out with the largest sample and high positive rate (66 %). Listeners cite noticeable improvement and rapid settle (5‑10 min). Music love is strong (≈50 %). AutoGluon predicts 6.86, modestly below observed, indicating outperforming predictions. YouTube views (30) are highest, though retention data are missing. Distribution approximates normal.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble helps most listeners feel calmer quickly, and many love the music; it’s the most watched protocol so far.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) shows solid efficacy (65 % positive). Listeners note a 10‑minute settle and mixed music sentiment. AutoGluon’s overstim prediction (6.86) aligns closely. YouTube views are low (5). Distribution is slightly left‑skewed.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall often calms listeners, though opinions on the music are mixed and it takes about ten minutes to settle.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) maintains a consistent overstim benefit (64 % positive). Qualitative feedback mentions balanced rhythm and rapid settle (5‑10 min). AutoGluon prediction (6.86) is marginally below the mean. YouTube views (8) are limited. Distribution appears unimodal.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier assists with overstimulation, with many finding it both balanced and calming.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) yields the highest anxiety‑zone performance, with 63 % positive outcomes. Qualitative feedback emphasizes moderate‑to‑noticeable improvement and rapid settle (5‑10 min). Music love is high (≈46 %). AutoGluon predicts 7.84, aligning closely, while YouTube views are low (7), indicating under‑discovery. Distribution shows slight right tail.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow consistently eases anxiety for most listeners within the first few minutes, and the music is well‑liked.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) records a modest anxiety benefit with 67 % positive ratings. Listeners note mixed settle times (5‑20 min) and a balanced rhythm perception. Music sentiment is split between liked and neutral, reflecting variable engagement. AutoGluon does not flag a substantial deviation from the observed mean. YouTube data are minimal (6 views). Distribution appears unimodal.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains a comparable anxiety effect; 61 % report positive change. Qualitative comments show some delayed settle (up to session end) and a strong preference for very‑settling rhythm. The outcome distribution shows a modest tail toward lower scores, hinting at possible bimodality. AutoGluon’s predicted anxiety score (7.84) exceeds the mean, implying latent potential. YouTube views are very low (4).

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) addresses acute distress; despite a lower mean, 53 % report positive change and 33 % significant improvement. Fast settle (≤5 min) matches crisis design. AutoGluon predicts 7.96 for crisis, suggesting a potential efficacy gap. YouTube reach (6) is minimal. Distribution is modestly left‑skewed.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor can help during high‑stress moments, though some listeners find it less effective than hoped.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) improves on H-CR-L1 with 55 % positive outcomes and rapid settle (5‑10 min). Rhythm feedback leans very‑settling, and music opinions are split between neutral and loved. AutoGluon predicts a crisis score of 7.96 for this zone, indicating a possible efficacy gap. YouTube views are low (4). Distribution appears roughly symmetric.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) mirrors H-CR-L2 in mean but shows higher variance and a few negative music ratings. Immediate settle (<2 min) dominates, consistent with crisis intent. AutoGluon’s crisis prediction (7.96) again exceeds observed scores, flagging a potential under‑performance. YouTube views are 5. Distribution shows a slight right tail, hinting at a subpopulation with lower scores.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) provides robust sleep benefits (65 % positive) with very fast settle (~5 min). Music is well‑liked. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for sleep, slightly above observed, suggesting modest improvement potential. YouTube views (4) are low. Distribution shows a gentle right‑skew.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern helps people relax quickly for sleep or rest, and most enjoy the music.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) presents a comparable sleep effect with 67 % positive, but the listener mix includes many caregivers. Notably, 3 respondents label rhythm as “too busy,” suggesting a mismatch for sleep intent. AutoGluon’s sleep prediction (7.54) exceeds the observed mean, pointing to possible improvement avenues. YouTube views (5). Distribution is tighter, hinting at a homogeneous response.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock works for some caregivers but a few feel the rhythm is too busy for sleep.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) sustains sleep benefits (64 % positive) with fast settle (≈5 min). Rhythm feedback includes the highest “slightly busy” count among sleep protocols (7), signaling potential entrainment friction. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly above observed. YouTube views (3). Distribution is roughly normal.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight generally aids relaxation fast, though a few listeners note a slightly busy rhythm.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) shows moderate focus gains (56 % positive). Rhythm feedback is unusually “slightly busy” (9), possibly dampening efficacy. AutoGluon predicts focus score 7.73, higher than observed, flagging a potential gap. YouTube views (5). Distribution is relatively wide, hinting at bimodality.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass can improve focus, though some find the rhythm a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) raises focus outcomes (66 % positive) with balanced rhythm and mixed music sentiment. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, modestly above the mean, indicating modest headroom. YouTube views (4). Distribution is moderately spread.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint helps focus for many, with balanced rhythm and mixed music feelings.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) achieves the highest focus score across all protocols (71 % positive). Qualitative data emphasize significant improvement and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, virtually matching observed performance. YouTube reach (15) is the strongest in THRIVING. Distribution is broad, hinting at bimodality.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit delivers a strong focus boost and many people report a big improvement, making it a standout protocol.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) yields modest creative‑flow gains (53 % positive). Qualitative data emphasize slight improvement and mixed settle times. AutoGluon predicts a creative‑flow score of 6.75 (close). YouTube reach is very low (2 views). Distribution shows slight left‑skew.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember offers a modest creativity lift, but results vary among listeners.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) improves on L1 with 58 % positive and significant improvement reports. Settlement leans toward 10 min. AutoGluon’s prediction (6.75) is marginally below, indicating slight under‑performance. YouTube views (2). Distribution is tighter.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current often enhances creative flow, especially during commuting or before sleep.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) achieves the highest creative‑flow mean; 67 % report noticeable or significant improvement. Settle times split 5‑10 min. AutoGluon predicts 6.75, under‑estimating observed performance, suggesting a strong outlier. YouTube views (7). Distribution is narrow, indicating consistent benefit.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora gives a strong creativity boost for many, though experiences differ.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) offers maintenance benefits (64 % positive) but shows unusually long settle times (~20 min) for many listeners. AutoGluon predicts maintenance score 7.69, slightly above observed. YouTube views (3). Distribution is tight, indicating consistent response.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone supports daily balance but many need a longer time to feel its effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) shows lower maintenance efficacy (59 % positive) with high variance and mixed rhythm feedback. AutoGluon predicts a maintenance score of 7.69, indicating a sizable gap. YouTube views (4) are limited. Drift (sim=0.637) suggests analytic instability.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis shows mixed results for balance, with some listeners feeling the rhythm is slightly busy.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) shows 71 % positive despite modest mean; however, 4 respondents reported “did NOT settle,” raising concern. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, exceeding observed, suggesting under‑performance. YouTube views (3). Drift is highest (sim=0.557), indicating evolving interpretation.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit generally helps maintain balance, though a few listeners didn’t settle at all.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) provides moderate mood‑energy gains (54 % positive) with mixed music sentiment and long settle times (≈20 min for many). AutoGluon predicts a low‑affect score of 8.23, indicating a sizable predictive gap. YouTube views (8) are modest. Distribution is fairly broad.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze offers moderate mood and energy lifts, though some listeners take longer to feel it.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) improves slightly (64 % positive) with strong music love and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon’s low‑affect prediction (8.23) again exceeds observed, suggesting optimization potential. YouTube views (8). Distribution is moderate.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise generally lifts mood and energy, with many listeners enjoying the music.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) shows reduced mood‑energy efficacy (56 % positive) and a notable “no effect” subgroup. Rhythm is evenly split between very calming and balanced. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a substantial gap. YouTube views (4). Distribution is slightly left‑skewed.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft gives a mixed mood boost; some feel it works well, others see no change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) underperforms in motivation (45 % positive) with considerable variance, suggesting divergent sub‑groups. AutoGluon predicts low‑affect benefit of 8.23 for X-ME-L1, far above observed, indicating under‑performance. YouTube views (5) are scarce.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath shows potential for motivation but many listeners feel it could be better tuned.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) records the lowest pct_pos (43 %) in the series, with modest qualitative improvement. Rhythm is balanced with some “slightly busy” notes. AutoGluon predicts low‑affect benefit 8.23 (X-ME-L1), again above observed. YouTube views (7).
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail shows modest motivation improvement, with many listeners loving the music.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) reverses the L1/L2 trend, achieving a higher mean and pct_pos (62 %) despite high variance. Qualitative feedback is moderate, and settle times are fast. AutoGluon does not flag this protocol for low‑affect zones, suggesting it may be an unexpected L3 advantage. YouTube views (7).
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback surprisingly boosts motivation for many despite its short length.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) yields modest mind‑clarity benefit (54 % positive). Two listeners reported worsening effects, a unique negative signal. Rhythm is balanced, music love high. AutoGluon predicts mindclarity score 7.54 for X-MC-L2, implying X-MC-L1 under‑performs. YouTube views (8). Distribution is slightly right‑skewed.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern often improves mind clarity, though a couple of listeners felt it made things worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) improves marginally (56 % positive) with strong “very calming” rhythm feedback (12). AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for this zone, indicating a performance gap. YouTube views (12) are the highest within the MC series. Distribution is fairly tight.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight provides modest mind‑clarity benefits and many love its calming rhythm.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) records the lowest mind‑clarity outcome (50 % positive) with high variance, indicative of a bimodal response. Qualitative comments note noticeable improvement for many, yet scores remain low. Rhythm includes “too busy” feedback. AutoGluon routes mindclarity to X-MC-L2, highlighting a mismatch. YouTube views (8).
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos shows mixed results – some people feel better, others don’t; we need more feedback to understand why.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The three series display broadly comparable efficacy, with subtle distinctions aligned to their design intents.
• HEALING shows the highest mean (≈6.98) and the lowest variance, indicating stable performance across diverse entries.
• THRIVING matches HEALING in mean but exhibits pronounced peaks at L3 levels, especially in Focus & Clarity.
• TRANSFORMING trails slightly in mean (≈6.59) and shows the greatest spread, consistent with its low‑affect, ascending‑arc purpose.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level patterns echo the intended therapeutic arcs.
• HEALING maintains consistent DESCENT effectiveness, particularly for anxiety and overstimulation, with modest variance.
• THRIVING’s HOLD protocols excel in Focus & Clarity, while Creative Flow shows modest benefits that improve at higher levels.
• TRANSFORMING’s ASCENT designs yield mixed outcomes; Mind & Clarity and Motivation protocols display high variance and occasional bimodality.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from expected patterns, suggesting subgroup effects or design mismatches.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7) reports low scores despite balanced rhythm feedback, indicating possible polarity.
• T-MB-L1/L2 (sim≈0.56‑0.64) exhibit strong cross‑run drift and conflicting caregiver‑heavy qualitative feedback.
• H-SL-L2 shows a caregiver‑dominated listener mix and prolonged settle times (~20 min), mismatching its 10‑minute DESCENT design.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements could improve efficacy and reach.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Adjust Adviser routing for T-FC-L3 (n=35) to align its high observed mean (7.63) with predicted focus score (7.73).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Increase hold phase length for T-CF-L3 (n=18) to reduce drift (sim=0.586) and support its strong qualitative improvements.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Investigate population split in H-SL-L2; consider separate caregiver and self‑listener analyses.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Boost discovery of high‑efficacy but low‑view protocols (e.g., H-AX-L1, H-OS-L1) via targeted outreach.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Redesign rhythm density for X-MC-L3, addressing the “too busy” feedback and low mean.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal analytic instability for several protocols.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: T-MB-L3 (sim=0.557) and T-MB-L1 (sim=0.565) show the greatest drift, indicating evolving interpretation.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: THRIVING Maintenance protocols (T-MB‑L1/2/3) form a tight cluster, while TRANSFORMING Mind & Clarity (X-MC‑L1/2/3) appear isolated.
• Confidence note: drift and clustering are based on 2‑3 runs for 25 protocols; signals are informative but not definitive.

by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) provides moderate mood‑energy gains (54 % positive) with mixed music sentiment and long settle times (≈20 min for many). AutoGluon predicts a low‑affect score of 8.23, indicating a sizable predictive gap. YouTube views (8) are modest. Distribution is fairly broad.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) improves slightly (64 % positive) with strong music love and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon’s low‑affect prediction (8.23) again exceeds observed, suggesting optimization potential. YouTube views (8). Distribution is moderate.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) shows reduced mood‑energy efficacy (56 % positive) and a notable “no effect” subgroup. Rhythm is evenly split between very calming and balanced. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a substantial gap. YouTube views (4). Distribution is slightly left‑skewed.

by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble helps most listeners feel calmer quickly, and many love the music; it’s the most watched protocol so far.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos shows mixed results – some people feel better, others don’t; we need more feedback to understand why.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit delivers a strong focus boost and many people report a big improvement, making it a standout protocol.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath shows potential for motivation but many listeners feel it could be better tuned.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow consistently eases anxiety for most listeners within the first few minutes, and the music is well‑liked.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor can help during high‑stress moments, though some listeners find it less effective than hoped.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall often calms listeners, though opinions on the music are mixed and it takes about ten minutes to settle.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier assists with overstimulation, with many finding it both balanced and calming.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern helps people relax quickly for sleep or rest, and most enjoy the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock works for some caregivers but a few feel the rhythm is too busy for sleep.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight generally aids relaxation fast, though a few listeners note a slightly busy rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember offers a modest creativity lift, but results vary among listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current often enhances creative flow, especially during commuting or before sleep.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora gives a strong creativity boost for many, though experiences differ.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass can improve focus, though some find the rhythm a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint helps focus for many, with balanced rhythm and mixed music feelings.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone supports daily balance but many need a longer time to feel its effect.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis shows mixed results for balance, with some listeners feeling the rhythm is slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit generally helps maintain balance, though a few listeners didn’t settle at all.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern often improves mind clarity, though a couple of listeners felt it made things worse.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight provides modest mind‑clarity benefits and many love its calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail shows modest motivation improvement, with many listeners loving the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback surprisingly boosts motivation for many despite its short length.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze offers moderate mood and energy lifts, though some listeners take longer to feel it.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise generally lifts mood and energy, with many listeners enjoying the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft gives a mixed mood boost; some feel it works well, others see no change.
